title: Multi-channel EEG recordings during a sustained-attention driving task
description: This dataset comprises multi-channel EEG recordings from 27 healthy adults performing a sustained-attention driving task in a virtual-reality simulator. The raw dataset contains 62 EEG sessions (~82 hours total) acquired at 500 Hz using a 32-channel system (30 scalp + 2 mastoid reference electrodes) with event markers for lane-departure trials and steering responses. The data were collected between 2005 and 2012 and have been converted to BIDS-EEG format, providing a resource for investigating neural correlates of sustained attention, fatigue, and motor response during simulated driving.
methods_description: EEG was recorded using a Compumedics Neuroscan Scan SynAmps2 Express amplifier with a 32-channel Quik-Cap (Ag/AgCl electrodes) at 500 Hz sampling rate with 16-bit quantization. Electrodes followed a modified international 10-20 system (30 scalp + 2 mastoid references A1/A2). Electrode-skin impedance was maintained below 5 kOhm. Participants performed a sustained-attention driving task in a virtual-reality simulator mounted on a six-degree-of-freedom Stewart platform, driving a simulated highway at constant speed while responding to randomly induced lane-departure events. A 33rd channel recorded the vehicle's lateral position (0-255 quantization) synchronized with EEG acquisition.
